title: Asciline
tagline: ASCII art generation engine with a clean CLI and web interface.
description: Asciline converts images and text into styled ASCII art with configurable character sets, density mapping, and output formatting. Available as a CLI tool and a lightweight web interface.
story: Started as a one-night experiment to see how far image-to-ASCII mapping could go with just character density and contrast math. Ended up building a full engine with a CLI and web layer.
journey: Asciline was a late-night rabbit hole — I wanted to understand image-to-ASCII conversion at the math level, not just use a library. Once I had the density mapping working, the CLI came together fast. The web interface was a natural add-on so it wasn't just a terminal toy. It's the kind of project that's small enough to finish, but deep enough to actually learn from.
forWho: Developers, terminal enthusiasts, and anyone who wants to turn images or text into styled ASCII art programmatically or in a browser.

detailedSpecs.architecture: Pure Python core (no external ML dependencies). Pillow handles image loading and greyscale conversion. Density mapping uses a configurable character ramp. Flask serves a minimal web frontend.
keyPatterns: Configurable character ramp for density-to-ASCII mapping
CLI with argparse for batch processing
Flask web layer for browser-based input/output
detailedSpecs.deployTarget: Local CLI or any Python-capable host
